{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

revision.text:
<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

revision.text:
<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

revision.text:
{{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

{{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

{{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

revision.text:
{{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirakuni Shrine]] {{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445|1=Inaseiri-hiko no Miko}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirakuni Shrine]] {{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445|1=Inaseiri-hiko no Miko}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirakuni Shrine]] {{moved from|Shirakunino Shrine}} <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445|1=Inaseiri-hiko no Miko}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirakuni Shrine]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445|1=Inaseiri-hiko no Miko}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirakuni Shrine]] <!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirakunino Shrine | native_name = 白國神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Shirakunino Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.864391|134.703841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Shirakuni Shrine.jpg}}
{{nihongo|'''Shirakunino Shrine'''|白國神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Overview ==
Long ago, some eighteen hundred years in the past, {{ill|Inaseiribiko-no-Mikoto|WD= Q108044445|1=Inaseiri-hiko no Miko}} （稲背入彦命）, a son of Emperor Keikō （景行天皇）, descended from Yamato to this region. He established a palace in the land of Shirakuni （白國） and ruled the Harima region （播磨地方）.<ref name=":0" />
One day, his grandson Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命）’s wife, Takatomi-hime （高富媛）, suffered greatly during childbirth.<ref name=":0" />
Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） set up white ritual offerings （白幣） on the peak of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, enshrined Kaminatatsuhime-no-Mikoto （神吾田津日売命）, and prayed with single-minded devotion for a safe delivery. At that time, the goddess appeared and declared:
{{quote|I am Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売）. Your prayer has reached the heavens. I shall remain in this land for a long time, protect women, and grant them safe childbirth.}}
At that moment, the white offerings （白幣） soared high into the sky, and together with them, the goddess concealed herself.<ref name=":0" />
When Asomu-no-Mikoto （阿曽武命） returned to his residence, a male child had been safely born, and he gave thanks for the divine virtue bestowed.<ref name=":0" />
In gratitude for this divine blessing, a shrine building （社殿） was established at the foot of Mount Kuratani （倉谷山）, and Kaminatatsuhime （神吾田津日売） was enshrined there. This is said to be the origin of Shirakuni Shrine （白國神社）.<ref name=":0"> shirakunijinja.com/#yuisyo</ref>
